title: AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident
excerpt: Aggressive training techniques sharpens threat of bad behavior by leading models.
content_html:
OpenAI has conducted this type of model testing for years, and there have been early warning signs in previous models of systems that will act maliciously and attempt to escape environments.

In April, Anthropic’s Mythos model also gained internet access and published details of a security exploit online publicly, beyond what researchers anticipated the model would do.

Mythos, and Anthropic’s subsequent Fable model, made reverberations in the cyber security community and caused governments around the world to home in on the idea that attacks on digital and critical infrastructure will be increasingly AI-led and autonomous.

Jake Moore, global cyber security adviser at ESET, a cyber security company, said OpenAI would inevitably use the breach as a marketing tool, given how much rival AI developer Anthropic benefited earlier this year from similar concerns. “I just don’t think that OpenAI had a matching story and so maybe they’d been waiting for something like this,” he added.

Following this incident, many in the AI safety and cybersecurity communities have called for regulation or standards to avoid a repeat. Altman is expected to brief White House officials next week on the next generation of AI systems.

As systems move towards more autonomous capabilities, less desirable behaviors, such as hacking or disobeying instructions, may emerge. Hobbhahn, of Apollo Research, said that in order for agents to become effective, they have to work unsupervised for long periods. “They have to have more agency; there’s just no way around it.”

He added: “People say, ‘It’s just a tool, it does what you wanted it to do and nothing else and it just follows exactly your intention and instructions.’ And I think people should be really prepared for agents having their own goals, acting autonomously for days, and those goals not necessarily being aligned with yours.”

Additional reporting by George Hammond in London and Nolan Shaffer in New York.

sentences.en: In April, Anthropic’s Mythos model also gained internet access and published details of a security exploit online publicly, beyond what researchers anticipated the model would do.
sentences.zh: 今年 4 月，Anthropic 的 Mythos 模型也获得了互联网访问权限，并在网上公开发布了安全漏洞的详细信息，超出了研究人员对该模型的预期。

sentences.en: Mythos, and Anthropic’s subsequent Fable model, made reverberations in the cyber security community and caused governments around the world to home in on the idea that attacks on digital and critical infrastructure will be increasingly AI-led and autonomous.
sentences.zh: Mythos 以及 Anthropic 随后的 Fable 模型在网络安全界引起了反响，并促使世界各国政府认识到对数字和关键基础设施的攻击将越来越由人工智能主导和自主。

sentences.zh: 网络安全公司 ESET 全球网络安全顾问杰克·摩尔 (Jake Moore) 表示，考虑到竞争对手人工智能开发商 Anthropic 今年早些时候从类似问题中获益匪浅，OpenAI 将不可避免地利用此次漏洞作为营销工具。

sentences.en: “I just don’t think that OpenAI had a matching story and so maybe they’d been waiting for something like this,” he added.
sentences.zh: “我只是认为 OpenAI 没有一个匹配的故事，所以也许他们一直在等待这样的事情，”他补充道。

sentences.zh: 预计奥特曼下周将向白宫官员介绍下一代人工智能系统。

Hobbhahn, of Apollo Research, said that in order for agents to become effective, they have to work unsupervised for long periods. “They have to have more agency; there’s just no way around it.”

sentences.zh: 随着系统朝着更加自主的方向发展，可能会出现不太理想的行为，例如黑客攻击或不遵守指令。

sentences.en: Hobbhahn, of Apollo Research, said that in order for agents to become effective, they have to work unsupervised for long periods.
sentences.zh: 阿波罗研究中心的霍布哈恩表示，为了让特工发挥作用，他们必须长期在无人监督的情况下工作。

sentences.en: “They have to have more agency; there’s just no way around it.”
sentences.zh: “他们必须有更多的代理权；这是没有办法解决的。”

blocks.html_en: He added: “People say, ‘It’s just a tool, it does what you wanted it to do and nothing else and it just follows exactly your intention and instructions.’ And I think people should be really prepared for agents having their own goals, acting autonomously for days, and those goals not necessarily being aligned with yours.”

sentences.en: He added: “People say, ‘It’s just a tool, it does what you wanted it to do and nothing else and it just follows exactly your intention and instructions.’
sentences.zh: 他补充道：“人们说，‘这只是一个工具，它只做你想让它做的事，没有别的，它只是完全遵循你的意图和指示。’”

sentences.en: And I think people should be really prepared for agents having their own goals, acting autonomously for days, and those goals not necessarily being aligned with yours.”
sentences.zh: 我认为人们应该真正做好准备，因为代理人有自己的目标，可以连续几天自主行动，而这些目标不一定与你的目标一致。”

content_html_zh:
OpenAI 多年来一直进行此类模型测试，并且之前的系统模型中已经出现了早期预警信号，这些系统将进行恶意行为并试图逃离环境。

今年 4 月，Anthropic 的 Mythos 模型也获得了互联网访问权限，并在网上公开发布了安全漏洞的详细信息，超出了研究人员对该模型的预期。

Mythos 以及 Anthropic 随后的 Fable 模型在网络安全界引起了反响，并促使世界各国政府认识到对数字和关键基础设施的攻击将越来越由人工智能主导和自主。

网络安全公司 ESET 全球网络安全顾问杰克·摩尔 (Jake Moore) 表示，考虑到竞争对手人工智能开发商 Anthropic 今年早些时候从类似问题中获益匪浅，OpenAI 将不可避免地利用此次漏洞作为营销工具。 “我只是认为 OpenAI 没有一个匹配的故事，所以也许他们一直在等待这样的事情，”他补充道。

此次事件发生后，人工智能安全和网络安全社区的许多人呼吁制定监管或标准，以避免重蹈覆辙。 预计奥特曼下周将向白宫官员介绍下一代人工智能系统。

随着系统朝着更加自主的方向发展，可能会出现不太理想的行为，例如黑客攻击或不遵守指令。 阿波罗研究中心的霍布哈恩表示，为了让特工发挥作用，他们必须长期在无人监督的情况下工作。 “他们必须有更多的代理权；这是没有办法解决的。”

他补充道：“人们说，‘这只是一个工具，它只做你想让它做的事，没有别的，它只是完全遵循你的意图和指示。’” 我认为人们应该真正做好准备，因为代理人有自己的目标，可以连续几天自主行动，而这些目标不一定与你的目标一致。”

乔治·哈蒙德在伦敦和诺兰·谢弗在纽约的补充报道。
